essence: David and Angela review her 21 new incident-analysis prompts (8 predicates + 8 observations), establish the three-part predicate/observation/classification structure, agree observations must be signals never questions, decide enhancement prompts can be deprecated, and concede human-in-the-loop confirmation is mandatory for market (incident reports are legal documents) while heavily using background agents to audit and improve POEM workflows.

actions.text: Adopt three-part structure per predicate: predicate (true/false), observation (narrative), classification (finite labels e.g. severity)
Update Oscar agent to understand LLM parallel and conditional-parallel workflow steps (7a/7b/7c chaining) and feed changes back to the original POEM framework
Deprecate the UI enhancement prompts once the predicate/observation system is stable
Build human-in-the-loop confirmation in the application (worker accepts accountability for AI text) before broad market release
Research classification prompt templates and fix observation template so outputs are never phrased as questions
Write handover/transference document for Angela's next session and commit+push
